exits.label: ◂ back to the wake and its songs

npcs.name: Old Mireth
npcs.title: the last who knows the deathsong
npcs.hail: *(an ancient shade sits very straight on a worn stone, humming a thread of melody so old and so quiet it seems to come from the reeds themselves, and when your cold falls across the place the humming stops, and the old eyes open, and fix on you with the particular sharpness of a master who has been waiting, without much hope, for a student worth the last of a dying art)*

npcs.name: the Unsung
npcs.title: a soul the song will not take
npcs.hail: *(a soul sits apart with its hands over its ears — not against noise, but against the very idea of being sung — and it watches you approach with the flinching dread of a thing that has decided, all the way down, that it does not deserve the one mercy the cantor offers, and would rather fade un-sung than be lifted by a song it is certain it has not earned)*

npcs.name: Pen
npcs.title: who is learning, and doubts
npcs.hail: *(a young shade practices the deathsong under its breath, getting the notes nearly right and the heart of it not at all, and there is a furrow of doubt on its earnest face — the doubt of a student who has learned the melody but not yet believed it does anything, and is beginning to suspect it has given its death to the study of a beautiful, useless thing)*

variants.text: *(The old shade's sharp eyes soften as they read the firelight of the wake still on you.)* …You've come from *Tib's* ring. *(a thread of melody under the words)* The *wake.* The *singing.* *(a thin, knowing smile)* *Tib* *Garron* throws a *grand* *noise*, bless his *loud* old heart — but *noise* isn't *song*, child, and a *wake's* *roar* isn't a *deathsong*, however *kindly* meant. *(it pats the stone beside it)* *Tib* sings to *cheer* the *living-dead.* *I* sing to *send* the *dead* *on.* *(the old eyes hold you)* They're *cousins*, the two arts — both are *witness* made *music* — but *mine* is the *older*, and the *quieter*, and the *dying*, because it takes a *lifetime* to *learn* and *nobody* lives long enough *down* here to *want* to. *(it studies your cold)* …Except, perhaps, a soul that *walks* *both* *ways* through the *door. Sit*, Returned. I have *one* song *left* to *teach*, and I have been *waiting* a *very* *long* *while* for *hands* *cold* enough to *carry* it.

*(The ancient shade lets the last thread of its humming fade, and regards you with eyes that have weighed a great many souls toward their rest.)* …A *cold* one. *(a slow nod)* *Good.* The *warm* ones can't *learn* it — they *flinch* from the *dead* at the *very* *moment* the *song* needs them *closest.* *(it pats the worn stone)* Sit, if you'll *humour* an *old* *cantor.* I am *Mireth.* The *last* — *truly* the *last*, I've *outlived* every *other* — who knows the *deathsong.* *(a thread of melody)* Not a *hymn*, child, not a *dirge* — those are for the *living*, to *comfort* *themselves.* The *deathsong* is *for* the *dead*: the *old* art of *singing* a *soul* *out* of the *grey* and *through* the *good* *door*, by *singing* it *so* *true* — its *name*, its *life*, its *whole* *self* — that it *knows*, at *last*, it was *witnessed*, and a *witnessed* *soul* can *let* *go.* *(the old eyes sharpen)* It is *dying* with me, this *art.* *Nobody* *learns* it — too *long*, too *hard*, too *sad.* *(a thin smile)* But *you.* *You* might. *Sit.* Let an *old* fool *teach* the *one* thing it *knows* to the *one* soul in an *age* who could *carry* it *further* than the *grave.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mireth
nodes.text: *(It folds its ancient hands, the melody still humming somewhere under its breath, and waits with the patience of a soul that has all the time the dead are given.)* Ask, child. *(a thread of song)* Or *say* you'll *learn.* I'll not *force* the *teaching* on a soul that didn't *come* for it — but I'll *warn* you, the *art* is *dying*, and you've the *one* *throat* I've *met* in a *century* that could *hold* it. *Choose* with *that* in *mind.*

choices.text: "Teach me, then. The deathsong. If it's witness made music, and witness is the one thing I've learned to do — I'd carry it. Show me how it's sung."
"What is it, really, Mireth? Not the legend — the mechanism. How does a song send a soul through a door that gods built to stay shut?"
"There's a soul over there with its hands over its ears — the Unsung. The song won't take on it. Why? A dying art should at least work on the willing."

*(the one who carries the dead, offering to carry the song)* "Here's the truth, Mireth: a craft that dies with its last master isn't a craft, it's a grave. You've spent a death afraid the deathsong dies with you. It doesn't have to. I go both ways through the door — I can carry your song to where you never could, sing it to souls on the *far* side of the Wall. Teach me, and the art doesn't end with the last cantor. It ends with the one student who could take it everywhere."
"Sing me something, Mireth. Before I decide. Let me hear what's dying."
"Keep your song a while, Mireth. I'll come back to learn it."

nodes.speaker: Old Mireth
nodes.text: *(The old eyes fill, and the ancient shade straightens with the joy of a master finally given a student.)* …You'll *learn* it. *(barely)* *Gods.* After all this *time.* *(it takes your cold hands in its translucent ones)* *Listen*, then, child, for the *teaching* is *short* — the *art* is *deep*, but the *secret* is *small*, and *that's* why it *dies*: souls *come* *expecting* a *grand* *technique* and *leave* when they *hear* how *simple* the *true* of it is. *(a thread of melody)* The *deathsong* is *not* a *spell.* There are *no* *magic* *notes.* *(it meets your eyes)* The *deathsong* is *this*: you *learn* a soul *true* — its *name*, its *life*, the *one* *thing* it *most* *needs* *witnessed* — and then you *sing* *that* *back* to *it*, in *whatever* *melody* the *truth* *wants*, until the *soul* *hears* its *own* *life* *sung* by *another*, and *knows*, *finally*, *down* to the *root*, *'I* *was* *real.* I *was* *seen.* I can *let* *go.'* *(it squeezes your hands)* *That's* *all* it is, child. *Attention*, made *melodic.* *Witness*, given a *tune* so the *soul* can't *look* *away* from *being* *loved.* *(fierce, weeping)* The *hard* part isn't the *singing.* *Anyone* can *carry* a *tune.* The *hard* part is *learning* the *soul* *true* *first* — and *you*, child, *you've* spent the *whole* causeway *doing* *exactly* *that.* *(it presses something — a knowing, a melody — into your cold hands)* *There.* You *have* it. The *last* *deathsong.* *Go* *sing* a *soul* *home.* Start with the *Unsung.* It's the *hardest* — but if you can *learn* *that* one *true*, you can *sing* *anyone.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mireth
nodes.text: *(It nods, approving the question.)* …The *mechanism.* *(a thread of song)* *Good.* You *want* the *how*, not the *legend.* *(it leans in)* Here it *is*, child, plain: the *good* *door* — the one a *rested* soul *moves* *on* *through* — is *not* *locked* by the *gods.* The *Wall* *forces* souls *down*; the *good* *door* *only* *opens* for souls that are *ready*, and *readiness* is *not* a *thing* a *god* *grants* — it is a *thing* a *soul* *reaches* when it *believes*, *truly*, that its *life* *meant* something, that it was *seen*, that it can *set* the *weight* *down.* *(it spreads its hands)* *Most* souls *never* *reach* it. They *die* *un-witnessed*, *un-sung*, *certain* they were *nothing* — and an *un-witnessed* soul *cannot* *believe* it's *ready*, so the *door* *stays* *shut*, and it *lingers*, and *fades.* *(the old eyes hold you)* The *deathsong* *reaches* that *readiness* *for* a soul that *couldn't* *reach* it *alone.* You *sing* it its *own* *life*, *true* and *whole*, and the *singing* *does* what a *lifetime* of *being* *overlooked* *prevented*: it *convinces* the *soul* it *was* *real.* And a *soul* that *believes* it was *real* *can* *open* the *door.* *(quietly)* That's the *mechanism*, child. *No* *magic.* Just the *oldest* *truth* there *is*: a *soul* *sung* *true* by *another* is a *soul* that *finally* *believes* it *existed* — and *that* *belief* is the *only* *key* the *good* *door* *ever* *needed.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mireth
nodes.text: *(The old face creases with an ancient, particular grief.)* …The *Unsung.* *(a long sigh)* *That* one *breaks* the *art's* *heart*, child. *(it looks toward the soul with its hands over its ears)* The *deathsong* works on *any* soul that *wants* to *believe* it was *real.* *Most* do — they're *starving* for it. *(it shakes its head)* But *that* one has decided, *all* the *way* *down*, that it *deserves* to *fade.* That its *life* was *not* *worth* a *song.* That to be *sung* *out* *gently* would be a *lie*, a *mercy* it has *not* *earned.* *(quietly)* And a *deathsong* *cannot* *force* a *soul* to *believe* it was *real* — it can only *help* a *soul* *reach* a *belief* it *already*, *secretly*, *wants.* A soul that *refuses* the *belief*, that *clutches* its *worthlessness* like the *one* *true* *thing* it *owns* — *(it spreads its hands, helpless)* — *that* soul I *cannot* *sing.* *No* cantor *can.* *(the old eyes find you)* Unless — *(a flicker of hope)* — unless a *soul* could *first* *learn* *why* it *believes* it *deserves* to *fade*, and *sing* *that* *true* *too — sing* the *very* *wound* that *makes* it *refuse* the *song.* *(quietly)* I'm *too* *old*, child. I haven't the *years* *left* to *learn* a *soul* that *deep.* But *you* — *you've* *learned* *harder* souls than *that* on *this* *causeway.* *If* you *take* the *deathsong*, *that's* your *first* and *hardest* *test*: *sing* the *one* soul that's *certain* it *can't* be *sung.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mireth
nodes.text: *(The ancient shade goes utterly still — and then a sound comes out of it that is half a sob and half the first bar of a song it had given up hope of ever passing on.)* …*'It* *ends* with the *one* student who could take it *everywhere.'* *(barely)* *Child.* *(it grips your cold hands hard)* Do you *know* what I have *feared*, *every* *day* of this *long* *death? Not* the *fading.* Not the *grey.* *This*: that I am the *LAST*, that the *deathsong* — *ten* *thousand* *years* of *cantors*, *every* one *teaching* the *next* — *dies* *with* *me*, in a *reed-bed*, *un-passed*, because *no* one *lives* long enough *down* here to *learn* it and *no* one *cold* enough *wants* to. *(it weeps)* And you've — you've *named* the *thing* I *couldn't* let myself *hope*: that the *art* doesn't *need* to *die* with the *last* *cantor* if the *last* *student* can *go* where *no* *cantor* *ever* *could* — *through* the *door*, to the *FAR* *side*, to the *walled*, the *fading*, the *souls* the *deathsong* could *never* *reach* because the *singer* could never *get* to *them.* *(fierce, radiant)* You could *sing* the *Faithless* *out* of the *WALL*, child. You could *carry* the *deathsong* *into* the *stone* *itself.* *(it presses the whole of the art into your cold hands)* *Take* it. *All* of it. *Every* *song* I *know* and the *secret* *under* them *all.* The *art* does *not* *die* *today.* It *changes* *hands* to the *one* pair *cold* *enough* to *carry* it *past* the *grave.* *(weeping, joyful)* *Go*, *last* *student.* *Sing* the *whole* *causeway* *home.* And when *they* *ask* who *taught* you — *(barely)* — *tell* them *Mireth.* *Tell* them the *art* *lived.* *Tell* them the *last* *cantor* *found* her *student* at the *very* *end*, and *did* *not* *die* the *last* *after* *all.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mireth
nodes.text: *(It closes its ancient eyes, and draws a breath it doesn't need, and sings — and the deathsong is not what you expected: not grand, not soaring, but small and plain and unbearably tender, a melody that sounds like it's naming someone, like it knows exactly who you are and is glad of it.)* *(You don't understand the old words. You don't need to. The song reaches into the cold you carry and finds the soul under it — the one that died, the one that came back, the one that's spent the whole causeway afraid it's a thing that shouldn't be — and it sings to *that*, gently, the way you'd sing a frightened child to sleep: there now. There now. You were real. You are real. You were always, always real.)* *(When it fades, you find your cold cheeks wet.)* *(Old Mireth opens its eyes.)* …*That*, child, is what's *dying.* *(quietly)* *Three* *bars* of it, on a *stranger*, and you *weep* — *you*, the *Returned*, the *thing* the *Wall* *fears.* *(a thin, knowing smile)* Because *even* *you* have *never* *once* been *sung.* *No* one *sings* the *cold* ones. We're too *frightening* to *love* *out* *loud.* *(it holds your gaze)* That's *what* you'd be *carrying*, if you *learned* it. The *one* mercy *no* one *ever* *offers* the *souls* who *need* it *most.* *Now* you've *heard* it. *Now* *choose.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mireth
nodes.text: *(It nods, and resumes its quiet humming, the melody threading back into the reeds.)* …*Come* *back.* *(between bars of song)* I'll *keep.* I've *kept* this *long.* *(a thin smile)* But *mind* the *word* *'long'*, child, in the *mouth* of the *last* of *anything.* I am *old*, and the *art* is *older*, and *neither* of us is *getting* *younger*, and the *day* I *fade* is the *day* the *deathsong* *fades* *with* me, *un-passed*, *ten* *thousand* *years* *ending* in a *reed-bed* with *no* *one* to *hear.* *(it hums)* So *come* *back* *soon*, if you're *coming.* Not for *me* — I've made my *peace* with the *grey.* For the *art.* For *every* *soul* that *could* be *sung* *home* and *won't* *be*, if the *song* *dies* with *its* *last* *cantor.* *(quietly, the melody soft)* *That's* the *only* *thing* I *fear*, child. *Not* the *ending* of *me.* The *ending* of the *song.* *Come* *back.* *Carry* it. *Don't* let *ten* *thousand* *years* *die* in the *reeds.*

variants.text: *(It sees the deathsong on you — the way you now carry it, learned and ready — and it presses its hands harder over its ears, shrinking.)* …*No.* *(barely)* No, you've — you've *learned* it. The *song.* I can *see* it on you, the *way* old *Mireth* *carries* it, and you mean to — *(it shakes its head, frantic)* — *don't.* *Please.* Don't *sing* me. *(its voice cracks)* I *know* what the *song* *does* — it *makes* a soul *believe* it was *real*, *worth* something, *ready* — and I am *NOT*, and a *song* that *tells* me I *am* would be a *LIE*, a *beautiful* *lie*, and I would *rather* *fade* *true* than be *sung* *home* on a *falsehood.* *(it meets your eyes, desperate)* *Whatever* you *learned* from *Mireth* — *whatever* *true* you *think* you can *sing* about *me* — you *don't* *know.* You *don't* *know* what I *did.* *Nobody* who *knew* would *sing* me *anywhere* but *down.*

*(It keeps its hands clamped over its ears as your cold approaches, and speaks too fast, too high, a soul talking to keep from being talked to.)* …I'm *not* — I don't *want* the *song.* *(barely)* You're *not* a *cantor*, you've not got the — but you're *cold*, and *cold* ones *meddle*, and I can *tell* you've *been* *talking* to *Mireth*, and *Mireth* *wants* to *sing* me *out* and I *WON'T* — *(it shrinks)* — *let* me *fade.* That's *all* I *ask* of *anyone.* *Let* me *go* *quiet*, *un-sung*, the *way* I — the *way* I *deserve.* *(it meets your eyes for half a second, then looks away)* *Don't* *make* me *into* a *thing* worth *singing.* I've *worked* very *hard* to *know* what I *am*, and a *kind* *song* would *un-do* the *one* *true* *thing* I've *got.*

nodes.speaker: the Unsung
nodes.text: *(It hugs its knees, hands still half-raised toward its ears, braced.)* …*What.* *(warily)* You're *still* *here.* *Most* *give* *up* on the *soul* that *won't* be *sung.* *(barely)* Say what you *came* to say, cold one. But I *warn* you — there's *no* *true* *song* in *me.* I've *checked.* *Every* *night.* There's *only* the *thing* I *did.*

choices.text: "What did you do, friend? You keep saying no one who knew would sing you. So tell me. I've heard the worst the causeway has — try me."

*(if you know the deathsong's secret)* "Mireth taught me the song's real secret: you don't sing a soul a lie about being good. You sing it *true* — and sometimes the truest thing isn't 'you were innocent.' It's 'you did wrong, and you've grieved it longer and harder than any judge would ask, and a soul that punishes itself this faithfully has already served the sentence.' I won't sing you clean. I'll sing you *finished.*"

*(the one who came back from the place it's headed)* "Listen to me. You want to fade because you think it's the sentence you deserve. But I've been where fading leads — the un-making, the grey nothing — and it isn't justice, friend. It's just erasure, and erasure doesn't balance a wrong, it only adds a second loss to the first. The soul you harmed isn't helped by you becoming nothing. Carrying it, awake, witnessed, is the harder sentence — and the only one that means anything."
"All right. I'll not sing you against your will, friend. But I'll sit with you a while, if you'll have the company."

nodes.speaker: the Unsung
nodes.text: *(It is silent a long while, and then the confession comes out flat and exhausted, a thing carried past the point of feeling.)* …I was a *physician.* *(barely)* A *good* one — that's the — that's what makes it *unbearable.* I was *GOOD.* *Saved* *hundreds.* *(its hands tighten)* And one *night*, *bone-tired*, *three* *days* *without* *sleep*, a *child* came in *fevered*, and I — I *misread* it. *Gave* the *wrong* *draught.* A *thing* a *first-year* *prentice* wouldn't have — but I was *tired*, and *proud*, and I *didn't* *check*, and the *child* — *(its voice breaks)* — the *child* *died* of *my* *mistake*, in front of its *mother*, who had *trusted* me, who *thanked* me, *even* *after*, because she *didn't* *understand* it was *my* *error* and I — *(barely)* — *I* *didn't* *tell* her. I let her think it was the *fever.* I let her *thank* me. And I *carried* it *alone*, and *kept* *practicing*, and *saved* *hundreds* *more* — but *every* one I *saved* was just — *just* me *trying* to *outrun* the *one* I *killed*, and you *can't*, you *can't* *outrun* it, it *runs* *exactly* as *fast* as *you* *do.* *(it looks at you)* *That's* what I *did.* I *killed* a *child* with *carelessness* and *let* its *mother* *thank* me. *Now* tell me there's a *true* *song* in *that.* *Tell* me what *melody* *fits* a *thing* like *that.* There *isn't* one. There's only the *fading*. And I *want* it. It's the *only* *honest* *thing* *left.*

nodes.speaker: the Unsung
nodes.text: *(It flinches as you begin — and then the song is not what it braced for. You do not sing it innocent. You sing it *true*: the good physician, the hundreds saved, the one terrible tired error, the child, the mother's misplaced thanks, the decades of silent penance — all of it, woven into a melody that does not excuse and does not erase, that holds the wrong and the grief in the same unbearable, witnessed chord.)* *(And the Unsung, hearing its own worst thing sung — not forgiven, not softened, but finally, fully *witnessed* — begins, terribly, to weep.)* …You — *(barely)* — you *sang* the *child.* *(it sobs)* You *didn't* *skip* it. *Everyone* who ever — the *few* I *told* — they *flinched*, or they *forgave* me *cheap*, or they *said* *'you* *saved* *hundreds*, *let* it *go'* — and *none* of it *touched* it, because *none* of it *SAW* it. *(it looks at you, shattered and, for the first time, lighter)* But you — you *sang* the *whole* of it. The *good* *and* the *killing.* The *saving* *and* the *silence.* You made it — *(barely)* — you made it a *life*, cold one. Not a *crime* I *am.* A *life* I *lived*, with a *terrible* *wrong* *in* it that I have *grieved* for *longer* than any *court* would *sentence.* *(it rises, fading-no-longer, the grey door's light growing)* …*'A* soul that *punishes* itself this *faithfully* has *already* *served* the *sentence.'* *(weeping, freed)* I've *served* it. *Decades* of it. And you're the *first* to *sing* it *whole* *enough* for me to *believe* the *sentence* could *ever* *end.* *(it looks toward the door)* I can *go.* Not *clean* — I'll *never* be *clean*, the *child's* *still* *dead.* But *finished.* *Witnessed.* *Sung.* *(barely)* *Thank* you, cold one. For *learning* the *worst* of me *true*, and *singing* it *anyway.* *That's* — *Mireth* was *right.* *That's* the *whole* *art.*

nodes.speaker: the Unsung
nodes.text: *(It lowers its hands from its ears, slowly, the first time, listening despite itself.)* …*Erasure* *doesn't* *balance* a *wrong.* *(barely)* It *only* *adds* a *second* *loss* to the *first.* *(it stares at you)* You've — you've *been* there. The *fading.* The *nothing.* I can *hear* it, the way you *say* it — not a *guess*, a *MEMORY.* *(its voice shakes)* I *wanted* it because — because *paying* felt *right*, and *fading* felt like *paying.* But you're saying — *(it struggles)* — you're saying the *child* I *killed* isn't *helped* by me *becoming* *nothing.* That *nothing* doesn't *pay* *anyone* *back.* It just — *un-makes* the *one* soul *left* who *remembers* the *child* *truly*, who *grieves* it *right.* *(it presses its hands to its face)* …*Gods.* If I *fade*, the *child* loses its *last* *mourner.* *Me.* The *one* who *can't* *forget* it.* *(it looks at you, stricken)* I thought *fading* was the *one* *gift* I *could* *give* it — my *own* *erasure*, a *life* for a *life.* And you're telling me the *child* would *rather* be *remembered* by the *flawed* *soul* that *killed* it than *forgotten* by a *nothing* that *paid* its *debt.* *(barely)* …Then I *can't* *fade.* Can I. *That's* — that's the *cruelest* thing you could have *told* me, cold one. That my *penance* was *another* *selfishness.* That *carrying* it *awake* is *harder*, and *truer*, and the *only* *sentence* that *honors* the *child* *at* *all.* *(it lowers its hands fully)* …I'll *carry* it. *Awake.* *Witnessed.* I'll *let* old *Mireth's* *student* *sing* me — *not* *home*, not *yet*, I'm *not* *ready* — but *into* *carrying* it *right*, instead of *running*, instead of *fading.* *(quietly)* You *turned* my *penance* *inside* *out, cold* one. It was *hiding.* You made it *witness.* That's — that's a *harder* *gift* than *fading.* *Thank* you for it. I *think.* *Ask* me again in a *century.*

nodes.speaker: the Unsung
nodes.text: *(It blinks at you, thrown by the offer — no song, no fixing, just company — and slowly, warily, lowers its hands from its ears.)* …*Sit* with me. *(barely)* You'd — *just* *sit?* Not *sing*, not *argue*, not *try* to *fix* — *(it studies you)* …*nobody* *does* *that.* They *come* to *save* me or they *leave.* *(it shifts, making room on the cold stone)* …*Alright.* *Sit*, then. *(a long silence)* …It's *strange*, cold one. *Less* *frightening* than I'd — when *no* one's *trying* to *sing* me *worth* *something*, I don't have to *defend* being *worthless* so *hard.* *(barely)* *Maybe* — *(it stops)* — *maybe* *come* *back.* *Sit* again, *sometime.* *Don't* *sing* me. Just — *sit.* I've been *alone* with the *thing* I *did* for so *long*, and *company* that *doesn't* *demand* I be *better* than it is — *(quietly)* — that's the *first* *kindness* that hasn't *felt* like a *lie.* *Maybe*, after *enough* *sitting*, I'll — I'll *tell* you what I *did.* And *maybe*, after *that* — *(it can't finish)* — *go* on, cold one. *Come* *back.* *Sitting's* — *sitting's* *allowed.* *Sitting* I can *bear.*

variants.text: *(The young shade breaks off its under-breath practice as you approach, and there's relief in it — someone to voice the doubt to.)* …Oh — you're the *cold* one *Mireth's* been *hoping* for. *(a wry, tired smile)* *Good.* *Maybe* *you* can *make* it *work*, because I *can't*, and I've been *trying* for — *(it gestures vaguely)* — *long* *enough* to *start* *wondering* if I *gave* my *death* to a *beautiful* *nothing.* *(it lowers its voice)* I'm *Pen.* *Mireth's* *prentice.* The *only* one — *nobody* *else* *wanted* to *learn* a *dying* *art.* *(it sighs)* And I've *got* the *notes*, cold one. *All* of them. I can *sing* the *deathsong* *perfectly* — *pitch*, *timing*, the *old* *words*, *flawless.* *(its face falls)* And it *does* *NOTHING.* I *sing* it at *fading* souls and they just — *keep* *fading.* *(barely)* So *either* the *art* is a *lie* old *Mireth* *believes* out of *love*, *or* I'm *missing* something, and I'm — I'm *starting* to *fear* it's the *first.* That I'm *learning* to *sing* a *song* that *doesn't* *do* anything but *sound* *beautiful* while souls *fade* *anyway.*

nodes.speaker: Pen
nodes.text: *(It worries at a thread of melody, getting the notes right and something else wrong, frustration plain.)* …You've *talked* to *Mireth.* *Tell* me, cold one — *honestly* — is the *deathsong* *real?* Or am I a *fool* who *gave* its *death* to a *pretty* *lie?* I'd — I'd *rather* *know.*

choices.text: *(if you've learned the secret)* "It's real, Pen — but you've got it backwards, and so did I until Mireth set me straight. You learned the *song* and skipped the *soul.* The notes aren't the deathsong. The deathsong is learning a soul *true* first — its name, its life, its wound — and then singing *that.* You've been singing a beautiful generic melody at strangers. No wonder it does nothing. You can't sing a soul home you never bothered to *know.*"
"Why'd you take it up, Pen? Of all the things a young soul could spend its death on — a dying art nobody else wanted. Why this?"

*(the witness who knows the art works)* "It's real, Pen. I've felt it — Mireth sang three bars at me and I wept, and I'm the thing the Wall fears. The song's not a lie. But it's not the notes, either. It's witness, and witness can't be faked or rushed or sung at a stranger. You've learned the surface of a deep thing. Don't quit the art. Learn the *depth.*"
"Keep practicing, Pen. The answer might be closer than you think."

nodes.speaker: Pen
nodes.text: *(It stares at you, and then its face does something complicated — chagrin and revelation and an enormous dawning relief all at once.)* …I *skipped* the *soul.* *(barely)* *Gods.* *(it sits down hard)* *That's* — that's *it*, isn't it. *That's* the *thing* *Mireth* *kept* *saying* and I *kept* *not* *hearing*, because I thought it was — *old-cantor* *talk*, *mystical* *nonsense*, *'sing* from the *heart'* — *(it laughs, shaky)* — and it was *LITERAL.* *Learn* the *soul* *true* *FIRST.* *Then* *sing.* *(it presses its hands to its face)* I've been — I've been walking up to *fading* *strangers* and *singing* a *perfect* *beautiful* *generic* *deathsong* at them like — like *reciting* a *prayer* at a *name* I *never* *learned*, and *wondering* why *nothing* *happened.* *(it looks at you, fierce with new understanding)* The *song* isn't the *art.* The *song* is the *LAST* *step.* The *art* is the *LEARNING* — the *sitting*, the *asking*, the *bothering* to *know* a *soul* *true* before you *ever* *sing* a *note.* *(it stands, transformed)* *That's* why *Mireth* could *sing* and I *couldn't.* *Mireth* *LEARNED* them *first.* *Always.* I just — *sang* *at* them. *(it grips your cold hand)* You've *saved* the *art*, cold one — *not* just by *learning* it, but by *teaching* it *back* to the *prentice* who'd *learned* it *wrong.* *(fierce, alight)* *Now* there's *TWO* of us. The *deathsong* doesn't *die* with *Mireth* OR with *one* *cold* *student* who'll *wander* *off.* It *lives* — in *me*, *here*, *teaching* it *RIGHT* to *whoever* *comes* *next.* *(weeping, grinning)* *Go* *tell* *Mireth.* *Tell* the *old* *fool* its *prentice* finally *heard* the *thing* it's been *saying* for *years.* *Learn* the *soul* *first.* *Gods.* It was *never* about the *NOTES.*

nodes.speaker: Pen
nodes.text: *(It softens, the doubt easing into something more wistful.)* …Why *this.* *(quietly)* I *died* *young*, cold one. *Very* *young.* *Fever*, like *half* the *dead* down here. And I came *down* *terrified* — *new*, *alone*, *certain* I was *nothing*, *fading* *before* I'd *barely* *arrived.* *(a thread of memory)* And old *Mireth* *found* me. At the *reed's* *edge, fading.* And it — *(its voice catches)* — it *sang* me. A *stranger.* A *frightened* *child* it had *never* *met.* It *sat* with me, and *learned* me — my *name*, my *little* *life*, the *one* *summer* I was *happy* — and then it *SANG* it, and I — *(barely)* — I *stopped* *fading*, cold one. The *song* *caught* me. It *told* me I was *real*, that my *little* *unfinished* *life* *mattered*, and I *believed* it because *Mireth* had *bothered* to *learn* it *true* *first.* *(it looks at you)* *That's* why *this.* The *deathsong* *saved* *me* — *literally*, *I* would have *faded* that *night* — and I thought — I thought if I *learned* it, I could *do* for *others* what it *did* for *me.* *(its face falls)* And then I *couldn't* *make* it *work*, and I started to *fear* maybe it *hadn't* *worked* on *me* either, maybe I just — *got* *lucky*, *clung* on, and *Mireth's* *song* was *just* a *nice* *sound* I *happened* to *survive.* *(barely)* …*Did* it? *Work* on me? Or am I a *fool* who *mistook* *kindness* for *magic?*

nodes.speaker: Pen
nodes.text: *(Its eyes go wide.)* …It *worked* on *YOU.* *(barely)* The *thing* the *WALL* *fears* — *wept* — at *three* *bars.* *(it sits back)* Then it's — it's *real.* *Truly.* *(relief floods its young face, then chases itself into fresh frustration)* But then *why* *can't* *I* — *(it stops, hearing what you said)* …*'Witness* can't be *faked* or *rushed* or *sung* at a *stranger.'* *(slowly)* I've been — *gods*, I've been *rushing* it, haven't I. *Singing* the *notes* *at* *strangers.* *Treating* the *song* like a *tool* you *point* and *fire.* *(it looks at its own hands)* And *Mireth* — *Mireth* never *rushed.* It *sat* with me a *whole* *night* before it *sang* a *note.* I thought that was just — *old* and *slow.* *(barely)* It was the *ART.* The *sitting* *was* the *art.* The *song* was just the *part* you could *hear.* *(it meets your eyes, fierce with new resolve)* You're *right*, cold one. I'll not *quit.* I've been *learning* the *surface* and *calling* it the *depth.* *(it straightens)* *No* *more* *singing* *at* *strangers.* From *now* I'll *do* it *Mireth's* *way* — *sit* *first*, *learn* *true* *first*, and *sing* *last*, *only* when I *know* the *soul* well *enough* to *sing* it *home.* *(quietly)* *Thank* you for not letting me *quit.* The *art* *saved* me *once.* I'd *hate* to *fail* it now, when I'm *one* *correction* away from *carrying* it *true.* *Go* on, cold one. *Tell* *Mireth* its *prentice* is *staying.* And *learning.* *Properly*, this *time.*

nodes.speaker: Pen
nodes.text: *(It nods, returning to its under-breath practice, but the doubt sits heavier now.)* …*Keep* *practicing.* *(a tired half-smile)* Aye. That's — that's the *one* thing I *know* how to *do.* *Practice.* *(it hums a bar, perfect and hollow)* *Perfect* *notes*, cold one. *Flawless.* And *not* *one* *fading* *soul* the *better* for them. *(it sighs)* *Go* on. *Talk* to *Mireth* — *learn* the *thing*, if you've the *throat* for it. *(quietly)* And if you *figure* out the — the *part* I'm *missing*, the *reason* the *notes* don't — *(it stops)* — *come* *tell* me. *Before* I *give* *up.* I *don't* *want* to *give* up, cold one. The *art* *saved* my *life* — my *death* — *whatever.* I just — I *can't* *make* it *save* *anyone* *else*, and a *song* that *only* *worked* *once*, on *me*, isn't an *art.* It's just a *thing* that *happened.* *(it returns to practicing, the notes perfect, the heart not yet there)* …*Come* *tell* me, if you *learn* the *secret.* *Please.*
